skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video.
skill.summary: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives from a Markdown documentation file. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are bash code examples in fenced code blocks demonstrating CLI usage. URLs are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh and Remotion docs. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration detected.

content.value_statement: Generate MP4 videos programmatically from React/Remotion TSX code without local rendering. This skill handles video encoding, rendering, and hosting through inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Render Remotion React components to MP4 video files
Support all Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, spring, interpolate
Configure video dimensions, FPS, duration, and codec
Pass dynamic props to React components for data-driven videos
Stream rendering progress updates during video generation
Handle complex animations with sequences and timing
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Video rendering time depends on duration and complexity
Network connection required for inference.sh service
Code must be valid TSX that exports a default component
Asset files (images, audio) must use publicly accessible URLs

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate animated video content for social platforms using code templates
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Data Visualization Videos
use_cases.description: Transform data into animated visual explanations with custom transitions
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and business intelligence teams

use_cases.title: Automated Video Workflows
use_cases.description: Batch generate videos from templates with dynamic data insertion
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and video production automation

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Remotion video with fade-in text that says 'Welcome' on a dark background. Use 3 seconds duration at 30 FPS with 1920x1080 resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Animated Counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video showing a percentage counter from 0% to 100% over 5 seconds. Use a spring animation for smooth easing. Format as 1080x1080 for square video.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Sequence Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video with three text elements that appear sequentially: 'First' at 0s, 'Second' at 1s, 'Third' at 2s. Each should fade in over 20 frames. Total duration 4 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Dynamic Data Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with props { title: 'Sales Report', subtitle: 'Q4 2024' } that displays these values in styled text. Use the interpolate function for smooth fade-in animation.

output_examples.input: Create a 3-second video with blue background and white text saying 'Hello World' that fades in
output_examples.output: MP4 video file (1920x1080, 30fps, 3 seconds) showing fade-in animation of white text on blue background, hosted on inference.sh CDN with direct download URL

output_examples.input: Generate a bouncing ball animation using spring physics over 2 seconds at 60 FPS
output_examples.output: Video file (1080x1080, 60fps, 2 seconds) with smooth spring-based bouncing animation, returned with public URL and metadata including duration and file size

output_examples.input: Render a data video with title 'Revenue: $50K' and subtitle 'Monthly Growth'
output_examples.output: Hosted MP4 displaying the provided title and subtitle with fade-in styling, includes video URL, duration (3s), resolution (1920x1080), and render completion status

best_practices: Test your Remotion components locally using the Remotion CLI before rendering at scale
Keep video duration under 10 seconds for faster rendering and lower costs
Use interpolate for smooth value transitions instead of manual calculations
Optimize images and assets before referencing them in your components
anti_patterns: Do not use local file paths for images or assets - all media must use publicly accessible URLs
Avoid excessively long durations or high FPS unless necessary - this increases render time significantly
Do not forget to export a default component - the renderer requires exactly one default export

faq.question: Do I need to install Remotion locally?
faq.answer: No. The rendering happens on inference.sh servers. You only need the inference.sh CLI installed to submit jobs.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The output is always MP4 format with H.264 encoding for maximum compatibility. You can configure resolution, FPS, and quality parameters.

faq.question: Can I use custom fonts or assets?
faq.answer: Assets must be referenced via public URLs. Use web fonts or import from CDNs. Local files are not supported in the remote rendering environment.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Render time depends on duration, FPS, and complexity. A simple 3-second 1080p video typically completes in 30-60 seconds.

faq.question: Is there a limit on video length?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but longer videos require more time and resources. Durations under 10 seconds are recommended for optimal performance.

faq.question: Can I render multiple videos in parallel?
faq.answer: Yes. You can submit multiple rendering jobs concurrently using the Python SDK or multiple CLI commands.
